title: Codereview pass 3: Identity - Good secure zeroing pattern
description: Good implementation worth defending: Identity class uses bytearray for mutable secret storage with in-place zeroing via _zero_bytearray(). The __del__ guard pattern is defensive and handles edge cases correctly. The module docstring properly documents Python's limitations around secure memory erasure. The wipe() method is idempotent. This is well-designed defense-in-depth for crypto key material in Python.
close_reason: Positive observation - secure zeroing pattern is good
